codeReview.summary: Re-review after fix commit 53552d6. Both blocking issues from round 1 have been correctly resolved: (1) PERIODICAL_NCX_CLASSES now matches Python exactly as {0:'section', 1:'article'} — the erroneous 'periodical' entry at depth 0 has been removed and tests updated accordingly. (2) EPUB2 <dc:creator> now includes opf:role='aut' attribute, with xmlns:opf namespace declared on the EPUB2 package element, and the EPUB3 package element correctly retains the marc: prefix without xmlns:opf. The fix also correctly separates the package element opening into EPUB2 vs EPUB3 branches. All 17 NCX/OPF tests pass, full epub test suite passes. Two pre-existing non-blocking issues remain (simplified check_epub_version logic, missing opf:scheme='uuid' on identifier) — these were noted in round 1 and are acceptable deferrals.

issues.description: EPUB4 check_epub_version logic is significantly simplified compared to Python (epub_output.py:654-683). Python's check_epub_version inspects HTML elements (article, aside, etc.), attributes (data-*, epub:*), MIME types, and layout flags to decide whether to downgrade from EPUB2. The Go code just uses the GenerateEpub2 boolean directly without any content analysis. This is an acceptable simplification for now since the Go caller sets GenerateEpub2 after performing its own analysis, but it should be documented that the full check_epub_version logic is not ported. (Carried over from round 1.)

Python (epub_output.py:872-873) adds opf:scheme='uuid' to the identifier element when in EPUB2 mode and the uid starts with 'urn:uuid:'. Go does not implement this EPUB2-specific identifier scheme attribute. (Carried over from round 1.)

addressesFailureFrom: Round 1 review found 2 blocking issues: (1) PERIODICAL_NCX_CLASSES had wrong entries {0:'periodical', 1:'section', 2:'article'} instead of Python's {0:'section', 1:'article'}. (2) EPUB2 dc:creator was missing opf:role='aut' attribute. Both were fixed in commit 53552d6 by fix-m1-scrutiny-blockers feature.
summary: PASS — Both blocking issues from round 1 are resolved. PERIODICAL_NCX_CLASSES now correctly maps {0:'section', 1:'article'} matching Python epub_output.py:56-59. EPUB2 dc:creator now includes opf:role='aut' with proper xmlns:opf namespace on the package element. The EPUB2 vs EPUB3 package element branching is clean: EPUB2 gets xmlns:opf without marc: prefix, EPUB3 gets marc: prefix without xmlns:opf. Tests updated to verify correct behavior and assert absence of wrong 'periodical' class. All 17 targeted tests and full epub suite pass. Two pre-existing non-blocking issues (simplified check_epub_version, missing opf:scheme='uuid') remain as acceptable deferrals.
